title: test_qwen3_vllm_locks.py stub breaks after Task 4 native-prompt _build_chat_prompt change
description:
Task 4 (feat/qwen-native-prompt-format branch) turned Qwen3VLLMBackend._build_chat_prompt
into an instance method that calls self._tokenizer.apply_chat_template(...) and reads
self._chat_template. Task 4's brief only authorized changes to
app/services/asr/qwen3_vllm.py, tests/test_chat_prompt.py, and
tests/test_vllm_mixing_fidelity.py (the latter's _backend() stub was updated to add
_tokenizer/_chat_template — same pattern needed here).

tests/test_qwen3_vllm_locks.py's _bare_backend() (and its _FakeTokenizer helper) was NOT
in that scope and was missed. Its _FakeTokenizer only implements encode()/decode(); it has
no apply_chat_template, and _bare_backend() never sets backend._chat_template. Any call to
backend._run_generate(...) or backend._decode_stream(...) via that stub now raises
AttributeError: '_FakeTokenizer' object has no attribute 'apply_chat_template'
inside worker threads (silently swallowed by the _hammer() helper — no join(timeout) catch),
so MainEngineLockTest.test_run_generate_serialized fails deterministically
(backend._llm.max_active ends up 0 instead of 1, since generate() is never reached).

Full-suite run on this branch after Task 4 (DEVICE=cpu .venv/bin/python -m unittest
discover -s tests): Ran 181 tests, FAILED (failures=1) -- only
test_qwen3_vllm_locks.MainEngineLockTest.test_run_generate_serialized.
test_decode_stream_and_run_generate_share_one_lock in the same file happens to still pass
only because its offline() threads die silently while ws() threads still exercise the real
lock -- that test is not exercising what it claims to anymore either.

Fix: mirror the test_vllm_mixing_fidelity.py _backend() pattern -- add
backend._tokenizer = PreTrainedTokenizerBase() equivalent that has a real (or fake)
apply_chat_template, or add a minimal apply_chat_template to _FakeTokenizer, plus set
backend._chat_template = _load_chat_template(tests/fixtures/qwen3_asr) in _bare_backend().
Must preserve the encode()/decode() overlap-recorder behavior the lock tests depend on.
close_reason: Fixed: _FakeTokenizer.apply_chat_template added (recorder-neutral, since it now runs outside _llm_lock by design); backend._chat_template set in _bare_backend(). 4/4 lock tests pass, 181/181 full suite green.

title: Diarization throughput: empty_cache guard + CAM++ pipeline pool
description: Implement spec docs/superpowers/specs/2026-07-17-diarization-throughput-design.md via plan docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-17-diarization-throughput-plan.md. Tasks 1-6 local (SDD, branch feat/diarization-throughput); Tasks 7 (VRAM measurement, BLOCKING gate for production N) and 8 (mixing test + bench) are H100-ONLY and gate the merge.

comments.author: Chandra L
comments.text: Local scope complete on feat/diarization-throughput (Tasks 1-6, 9 commits 1058a64..56daaab + amendments). Suite 156/156 green. Final whole-branch review (fable): READY FOR H100 GATE — zero Critical; one Important filed as qwen3-asr-9nk; cleanups as qwen3-asr-8vv. Branch must NOT merge until Task 7 (VRAM measurement, gates production DIARIZATION_POOL_SIZE) and Task 8 (mixing test + bench) verdicts are recorded. Ledger: .superpowers/sdd/progress.md

comments.author: Chandra L
comments.text: Three-reviewer validation pass (fable x3: concurrency/mutation/documents) complete, fixes in 8a97cb2: reload-wedge liveness defect closed (exclusivity invariant itself survived attack), 7 surviving mutants killed, plan Task 7 Step 2 made runnable (was dead-process; feeds the BLOCKING gate), lock-claim contradiction in spec+plan corrected, stale 127 baseline fixed. Suite 159/159; clean-tree determinism 30/30. Branch remains gated on H100 Tasks 7-8.

comments.author: Chandra L
comments.text: MERGED TO MAIN (f071ebe) and pushed, per user instruction, WITH THE H100 GATE STILL OUTSTANDING — same pre-gate-merge situation qwen3-asr-otx tracks for the context-wording branch. Issue stays open until Task 7 (VRAM measurement -> production DIARIZATION_POOL_SIZE; code default 4 NOT validated) and Task 8 (test_offline_mixing.py MANDATORY + bench vs 0.78 req/s / 12.8s / 4.299s) verdicts are recorded from the H100. Do NOT deploy a pool size >1 before Task 7's numbers exist.

title: Context-wording change merged to main WITHOUT the measurement gate — run it or revert
description:
Merge 6bae270 shipped the qwen3_vllm.py:81 wording change (named-entity framing -> general background framing) to main on the maintainer's explicit decision, WITHOUT running the plan's blocking Task 4 gate. No claim exists that the new wording steers as well as the old.

UNVALIDATED RISK: the model may echo caller context into transcripts instead of using it as a hint, degrading output for anyone who sends prompt/vocabulary_id/context. vLLM/CUDA only (Rust discards context). Failure mode is degraded transcripts, not a crash. Revert is one line at app/services/asr/qwen3_vllm.py:81.

TO CLOSE THIS, do ONE of:
(a) Run the gate. Instrument is ready: scripts/h100/bench_context_prompt.py. Runbook is Task 4 of docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-17-transcription-context-prompt.md. Needs >=10 real Indonesian/Chinese clips with HARD spoken named entities. Existing ASR_TEST_AUDIO_DIR fixtures will NOT do: they use common words (银行/机场/医院) the model already gets right with no context, so they fail the plan's headroom hard-stop and would produce a meaningless PASS.
(b) Revert the wording change.
(c) Consciously accept it permanently and close with that rationale recorded.

Note when running: compare's exit 0 is NOT sufficient. Wrapper-only instruction echo moves no keyword and is invisible to both blocking rules; the plan mandates reading a sample of the printed text= lines before recording a PASS.

title: Unsupported language returns HTTP 500 (DEFAULT_SERVER_ERROR) instead of 400
description: Found by final whole-branch review of feat/qwen-native-prompt-format. _normalize_language_name raises ValueError on unsupported language (qwen3_vllm.py:81) -> @_handle_asr_error wraps EVERY exception into DefaultServerErrorException 50000000 (qwen3_engine.py:88-96) -> get_http_status_code maps >=50000000 to 500 (app/core/exceptions.py:48-50). On WS the exception escapes the start handler, sends an error frame, tears down the connection. A client typo (language=xx) is a client error: 5xx pollutes server-error alerting and gives wrong retry semantics. Implementation matches the plan exactly — this is a plan-level gap, fails closed. Fix: catch ValueError at the API layer (OpenAI endpoint, Alibaba endpoint, WS start) and raise InvalidParameterException (40000003). NOTE for Task 5 operator: the language=xx-nonsense check in plan Task 5 Step 4 will observe a 500-class error payload — that is THIS issue, not a gate failure.

title: Implement qwen-native prompt format (plan 2026-07-17)
description: Execute docs/superpowers/plans/2026-07-17-qwen-native-prompt-format.md on branch feat/qwen-native-prompt-format. Tasks 1-4 local (template loading, fixpoint sanitizer, language validation, native prompt render + test rewrite). Task 5 is the container/GPU merge gate and cannot run on the dev box.

comments.author: Chandra L
comments.text: Tasks 1-4 complete on feat/qwen-native-prompt-format (082662b..c15bc27, 6 commits). Suite 182/182 green. Per-task reviews clean; final whole-branch review (fable): 'With fixes' -> sanitizer O(n^2) DoS fixed with pre-cap (c15bc27, RED 15.96s/GREEN sub-ms); 400-vs-500 language error filed separately. REMAINING: plan Task 5 container gate on GPU host (drift check must EXECUTE, skeleton parity, e2e incl. language=xx-nonsense -> expect 500-class payload = the filed issue, not a gate failure). DO NOT merge before Task 5. Ledger: .superpowers/sdd/progress.md

title: Diarization warmup failure at boot leaves lazy N-build stall on first request
description: Final-review Important on feat/diarization-throughput: preload_models swallows diarization warmup failure (model_loader.py records error string, service starts). First diarization request then triggers ThreadedEnginePool._ensure_queue from an executor thread: builds all N instances sequentially while holding an offline admission permit; up to VLLM_OFFLINE_CONCURRENCY-1 further requests block on _init_lock holding remaining permits, stalling ALL offline traffic behind one N-build (pre-existing lazy-singleton pattern amplified xN). Not a correctness defect; no deadlock. Fix options: make diarization warmup failure fatal at boot, or surface in /health. NOTE existing tests assert swallow semantics (test_preload_models_config_repair) — changing this is a deliberate behavior change, do not slip it into the throughput branch. Operator mitigation documented in plan Task 8 amendments: any CAM++ 加载失败 boot line is a hard stop.

title: MODEL_PATH slug shape is a latent env-var compat break
description:
_registry_from_entries (app/core/model_paths.py) gives a models.json entry with ONE declared model the bare key slug, and an entry with BOTH offline+realtime suffixed slugs (<KEY>_OFFLINE/<KEY>_REALTIME).

So adding a 'realtime' id to qwen3-asr-1.7b would turn an operator's working MODEL_PATH_QWEN3_ASR_1_7B into an unknown-slug BOOT FAILURE. It fails loud and never mis-resolves, so it is acceptable — but it is the only way this feature can break a working deployment. Anyone editing models.json needs to know.

From the feat/model-path-overrides final review.

title: Document that .env now reaches every entrypoint + Compose v2.24 prerequisite
description:
Three doc lines in README, from the feat/model-path-overrides final review:

1. load_dotenv moved from start.py to app/core/config.py, so 'python -m app.utils.download_models' and bare 'uvicorn app.main:app' now read .env for EVERYTHING (DEVICE, HOST, PORT, LOG_LEVEL, ASR_BATCH_SIZE...), not just MODEL_PATH_*. Sanctioned by the spec and the whole point, but it is NOT byte-for-byte prior behavior and no doc warns operators.
2. env_file in both compose files injects .env keys outside the 'environment:' block into the container. 'environment:' still wins for declared keys.
3. env_file with 'required: false' needs Docker Compose v2.24+ — now an undocumented deployment prerequisite.

title: get_slugged_assets re-lists asset groups instead of filtering
description: model_capabilities.py:189-197 re-lists the same four asset tuples that get_download_modelscope_assets() (:111-116) already composes. Add a 5th group and one function silently misses it. Fix: make it a filter over get_download_modelscope_assets(). Flagged by the feat/model-path-overrides final review as the roll-up item most likely to bite.

title: tests/test_huggingface_model_utils.py runs in no suite (script-style, 0 collected)
description:
The file uses bare 'def test_*()' + a __main__ block instead of unittest.TestCase. Consequences:
- 'python -m unittest tests.test_huggingface_model_utils' reports 'Ran 0 tests ... OK' — a vacuous pass that looks like a green guard.
- 'unittest discover -s tests' does not collect it either (102 tests collected, none from this file).
So the file only runs if invoked directly: 'python tests/test_huggingface_model_utils.py' (currently exit 0, genuinely passing).

Found during feat/model-path-overrides Task 3, where this file is the designated regression guard proving default HF resolution is untouched by MODEL_PATH overrides. The guard is real but automated by nothing.

Fix: convert to unittest.TestCase so discover collects it.

title: list_declared_entries checks HF ids against ModelScope cache
description:
manager.py builds Path(settings.MODELSCOPE_PATH) / config.offline_model_path, but offline_model_path for qwen3-asr-* is a HuggingFace id (Qwen/Qwen3-ASR-1.7B). That path never exists, so the /models API reports exists:false for Qwen models that are present and working in the HF cache.

Found while implementing per-model MODEL_PATH overrides (feat/model-path-overrides). Deliberately left alone there to keep that branch scoped; _declared_model_exists() (manager.py:36-46) only makes the override case report true, and its docstring records the quirk.

NEW CONTEXT from that branch: /models is now ASYMMETRIC. An OVERRIDDEN qwen model reads exists:true, while an equally-working HF-CACHED one still reads false. Arguably more confusing than the previous uniform false.

Fix: route the check through find_huggingface_snapshot_dir for HF-sourced ids. Note this changes existing API output for non-override users.

title: Post-merge cleanups from diarization-throughput final review
description: Fix-later Minors triaged by the final whole-branch review of feat/diarization-throughput: (1) add an assertLogs-style test pinning the VLLM_OFFLINE_CONCURRENCY > DIARIZATION_POOL_SIZE boot warning (currently only incidental log evidence; it is the branch's only operator-facing guardrail); (2) remove the unreachable 'pool_size >= 1' else-branch in model_loader.py warmup block (pool clamps to max(1,.), config validates >=1, failures raise into except — dead branch implies impossible state; plan defect carried in faithfully); (3) route the config.py invariant warning through the app's loguru logger instead of stdlib logging lastResort; (4) config comment should state the pool bound is the SUM of concurrent offline lanes across engine families, not just VLLM_OFFLINE_CONCURRENCY. Full accept/fix-later triage recorded in .superpowers/sdd/progress.md.

comments.author: Chandra L
comments.text: Validation pass addition: add a test pinning diarize's bind-once pool local (current ordering test mocks methods on the module-global pool, so a regression to double global resolution would pass). Also note suite gained a ~4.8s subprocess test (test pool sized from settings via DIARIZATION_POOL_SIZE=2).

title: vocabulary_id user docs still describe hotword-only semantics
description: User approved broadening context from 'entity list' to free-form background text. README.md:287, docs/README_zh.md:285, app/api/v1/asr.py:112,172 and app/models/asr.py:45 still describe vocabulary_id solely as 无权重热词上下文 / Hotword context. Not false (hotword lists still work) but the broadened semantics appear nowhere in Alibaba-protocol user docs. The spec's scope deliberately covered only the three OpenAI-endpoint sites. Staleness, not a lie.

title: bench_context_prompt: zero-tolerance leak rule can false-block on noise
description: The leak rule blocks on ANY increase (new_leak > old_leak). With a 0.0 baseline, a single stochastic foreign-keyword hallucination in ~120 observations fails the merge. Fails safe (blocks, never passes) and rerun is the remedy, but the Task 4 operator must be briefed. Plan Step 4 already tells them to inspect the printed text= before believing a leak. Also: --sentence-context is not checked for foreign keywords the way --topic-context is.

title: SDD brief template: openai_compatible import smoke check cannot pass on dev box
description: A task brief specified 'from app.api.v1.openai_compatible import router' as a verification step. It raises RuntimeError ('当前环境未找到可运行的 Qwen3-ASR 模型') on any box without a model installed — confirmed failing on unmodified pre-change code, so it is a defect in the brief, not a regression. py_compile + the full suite was an adequate substitute for a doc-only change. Any future brief reusing that smoke check has the same hole.

title: bench_context_prompt: run mid-flight errors exit 1, contract says 0/2
description: scripts/h100/bench_context_prompt.py: cmd_run's mid-flight failures (connection refused, HTTP error, unwritable --out) traceback to exit 1; its documented contract is 0=ok, 2=setup error. --iterations 0 also unrejected (writes zero-data file, then ZeroDivisionError). No gate defeat: compare rejects the zero-data file with exit 2. Found by final whole-branch review of feat/transcription-context-prompt.

title: Narrow bare except in check_model_exists to OSError
description:
download_models.py:69 wraps find_huggingface_snapshot_dir in 'except Exception: pass'. The final review verified this is currently UNREACHABLE with a bad MODEL_PATH override (is_overridden is the first statement of both check_all_models loops, so it raises first) — so this is not a live bug.

But the guarantee is POSITIONAL, not structural: reorder the loop, or call check_model_exists from new code, and the ValueError is swallowed again — a typo in MODEL_PATH_VAD would make the downloader report an unrelated model missing and re-download it. Nothing tests this.

The bare except exists to catch path.iterdir() on an unreadable dir = OSError. Narrowing to 'except OSError:' makes it structural for one character.

title: Test hygiene sweep: vacuous assert + Pyright reportArgumentType
description:
From the feat/model-path-overrides final review roll-up:

1. tests/test_model_path_overrides.py test_empty_directory_raises asserts assertIn('empty', str(exc)) but the temp dir is NAMED 'empty', so the substring matches the interpolated path regardless — it passes on all three error messages and verifies nothing. Fix: assert 'is an empty directory'.
2. app/core/model_paths.py:103 — real Pyright reportArgumentType: asset.slug is Optional[str] into _record(slug: str). Runtime-safe (get_slugged_assets filters None) but the comprehension can't narrow. Fix: local narrow at the call site.
3. download_models.py:278 calls fix_camplusplus_config() outside any try — under HF_HUB_OFFLINE=1 + read-only mount + export_dir set + nothing missing, the RuntimeError escapes as a raw traceback from a CLI where everything else prints a formatted warning. Cosmetic.
